avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. Over a wide area wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1600 m and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Slab avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of the moderate to strong northeasterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. The wind slabs are poorly bonded with the old snowpack. They are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of the last few days must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. These have formed especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. They can be released very easily. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Snow sport activities outside marked and open pistes call for experience and great restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh snow of last week and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. The sometimes deep wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant on steep shady slopes above approximately 2600 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The avalanches can as before reach medium size.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: The new snow of the weekend is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in some places.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day individual small to medium-sized wet avalanches are possible above approximately 1300 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of the last few days remain in some cases prone to triggering in particular on shady slopes.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls extremely steep terrain: The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
The backcountry touring conditions are generally favourable. Wind slabs require caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
On Sunday, at an altitude of around 1,500 metres, 50 to 100 cm of snow fell widely across the eastern and south-eastern parts of the region, and 30 to 40 cm of snow fell across the rest of the area. The weather conditions facilitated a substantial settling of the snowpack.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from the middle of the day to slight softening of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2000 m.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Sunday. Especially in the SE areas bordering Piedmont. The wind will be moderate to strong adjacent to ridgelines. As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent from the late morning. In addition sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in particular on northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2000 m. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from variable directions, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger.
avalancheActivity.comment: The wind slabs of the last few days remain in some cases prone to triggering. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Avalanches can in many places be released, mostly by large loads. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment: At low and mid-mountain altitudes, the various phases of melting and refreezing have generally favored the formation of a hard, load-bearing surface crust that may become wetter during the day. At higher altitudes, the snowpack is more variable, as these crusts, often load-bearing, alternate with localized accumulations of wind-drifted snow, which are generally clearly visible. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Light snowfall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m.
The easterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow. The various wind slabs have bonded generally well with the old snowpack. The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a strong wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs formed in the last two days. These are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally at elevated altitudes. Avalanches can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Mostly they are medium-sized.
Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from midday above approximately 1500 m. The wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible in the regions exposed to precipitation.
Distinct weak layers exist deep in the snowpack on shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in some places by individual winter sport participants especially here. Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be triggered in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions facilitated a gradual settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Distinct weak layers exist deep in the snowpack on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Up to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places. These can in some cases be triggered in near-surface layers and reach medium size, especially on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes above approximately 2500 m. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Isolated whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can be triggered in near-ground layers. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. Avalanches can in isolated cases reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall.

West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m:
Wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack.

Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular.

tendency.highlights: Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily in some places, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. They are clearly recognisable to the trained eye. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can be triggered in near-ground layers. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall.

West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m:
Wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack.

Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular.

tendency.highlights: Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above approximately 2000 m. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from variable directions, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases in particular on very steep shady slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Wednesday above approximately 1700 m. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. In some places wind slabs formed.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind slabs represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.
As a consequence of warming during the day individual moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some localities.

Shady slopes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Some snow will fall during the night in some localities.
